function.name: search_email
function.description: Search emails in your inbox. Filters can be combined (AND). Returns email items with email_id, from, to, cc, subject, date, is_read, is_starred, folder, attachments. Does NOT return body — use get_email_body for that.

content.description: Search term matching against both subject and body (full-text). Omit to return all emails matching the where filters.

where.description: Filter conditions — all optional and AND-combined.

from_.description: Sender email address to filter by.

starred.description: True for starred only, false for unstarred, omit for both.

is_unread.description: True for unread only, false for read only, omit for both.

sent_before.description: ISO date (e.g. '2026-01-01'). Filters emails sent before this date.

sent_after.description: ISO date (e.g. '2026-01-01'). Filters emails sent after this date.

order_by.description: Sort field. Prefix '-' for descending (e.g. '-date' for newest first). Defaults to '-date'.

connection_id.description: The ID of the email connection to search (e.g. 'eml_abc123'). Omit if only one connection is configured.

function.name: get_email_body
function.description: Fetch the full plain-text body of a specific email by its email_id.

connection_id.description: The ID of the email connection this email belongs to (e.g. 'eml_abc123'). Omit if only one connection is configured.

function.name: forward_email
function.description: Forward an existing email to a new recipient. The original message is quoted in the forwarded email.

body.description: Optional additional message to prepend above the forwarded content.

connection_id.description: The ID of the email connection this email belongs to (e.g. 'eml_abc123'). Omit if only one connection is configured.

function.name: trash_email
function.description: Move one or more emails to the Gmail Trash folder. Accepts a list of email_ids from search_email. Returns a short summary of what was trashed vs what failed.

email_ids.description: List of email_ids returned from search_email.

connection_id.description: The ID of the email connection these emails belong to (e.g. 'eml_abc123'). Omit if only one connection is configured.
